debug.notConnectedBody: Connect a board to watch STATUSTEXT messages and link stats.

placeholder.comingSoon: This page is a placeholder. The real implementation lands in a later milestone.

params.notConnectedBody: Connect to fetch the full parameter table. You can then search, edit, and write changes back with a diff preview.
params.discardedOnDisconnect: {{count}} unsaved edit(s) were discarded — parameter edits don't persist across sessions.
params.confirmLeave: Discard {{count}} unsaved parameter edit(s) and leave this page?
params.title: Full Parameter Table
params.errorNoResponse: The board didn't respond to the parameter request. Check the connection and try again.
params.errorMissing: {{count}} parameter(s) could not be read after several attempts.
params.errorDrift: The board's parameter count changed mid-fetch — the link may be unstable.
params.pendingBadge: {{count}} unsaved — the board still has the old values
params.noChanges: Editor matches the board — edit any value to queue a change.
params.errorPrecision: Value too large to store exactly
params.drawerSubtitle: Each value is written, then read back to verify. Failures stay listed so nothing is silently lost.
params.statusTimeout: No confirmation from the board (timed out)
params.statusBusy: Write already in progress for this parameter
params.statusPrecision: Value too large to store exactly — not sent

dashboard.notConnectedBody: Plug your board in via USB, pick its serial port in the top bar, and hit Connect. Telemetry, sensors and motors will light up here.

power.title: Power
power.noRemaining: Battery percentage unavailable — showing voltage only

charts.notConnectedBody: Connect a flight controller to chart telemetry live. Selected Series scroll through a rolling 60-second window; after a disconnect the last minute stays on screen for review.
charts.emptyBody: Pick one or more Series above — a chart appears for each unit as soon as something is selected.

setup.notConnectedBody: Frame type, ESC protocol, battery monitoring and failsafes are read from — and written to — the flight controller.
setup.subtitleNote: Nothing is written until you review & write
setup.subtitleBody: Each setting shows the ArduPilot parameter it controls. Changes queue in the bar below.

failsafes.sectionSubtitle: What the vehicle does when a link or battery fails

setup.discardedOnDisconnect: {{count}} unsaved setup change(s) were discarded — they don't persist across sessions.

firmware.title: Firmware
firmware.subtitle: Driverless flashing over Web Serial
firmware.connectNote: Connect a board to highlight its recommended firmware — every board is always listed, connected or not.
firmware.onlineErrorNote: You can still flash a local .apj file below.
firmware.onlineEmpty: No firmware entries in the mirror yet.
firmware.localDropHint: Drag a .apj file here, or click to choose one
firmware.localChecksumNote: Local files aren't checksum-verified (online downloads are).
firmware.confirmBody: The board reboots into its bootloader, is reflashed, then reboots back. Parameters are preserved. Don't disconnect the USB cable during this process.
firmware.cancelUnavailable: Can't cancel once erasing has started — see it through, or wait for it to finish and retry.
firmware.safeRetryHint: The board is still in its bootloader — retry is safe.
firmware.disconnectedHint: Connection lost. Reconnect the USB cable, then retry.
firmware.doneMessage: Flashed and verified — board rebooted, parameters intact.
firmware.dfuSoftwareBody: If the board still talks over USB, skip the BOOT button — reboot it straight into the STM32 system bootloader.
firmware.dfuEnterWarning: DFU flashing erases all parameters — back them up first (Parameters → Export).
firmware.dfuEnterDone: Rebooted into DFU. Now click "Select DFU device" and pick the board from the browser dialog.
firmware.dfuEnterUnavailable: Connect a novaX board that supports one-click DFU to unlock this.
firmware.dfuStep1: Unplug USB. Hold the BOOT button on the FC.
firmware.dfuStep2: Plug USB back in while holding — then release.
firmware.dfuStep3: Select the DFU device below, drop a *_with_bl.hex file, and flash.
firmware.dfuWaiting: Waiting for a board in bootloader mode…
firmware.dfuChipInfoError: Couldn't read the chip's flash layout: {{message}}
firmware.dfuZadigHint: Windows: if the device doesn't appear, install the WinUSB driver via Zadig (choose "STM32 BOOTLOADER", driver WinUSB), then try again.
firmware.dfuDropHint: Drag a *_with_bl.hex file here, or click to choose one
firmware.dfuChecksumNote: Local files aren't checksum-verified (online downloads are).
firmware.dfuConfirmWarning: This performs a FULL CHIP ERASE — the bootloader and all parameters are wiped, not just the app. Make sure this file matches the connected board.
firmware.leaveConfirm: A firmware flash is in progress — leaving now may brick the board. Leave anyway?

calibration.notConnectedBody: Accelerometer and compass calibration run live against the flight controller's sensors.
calibration.title: Sensor Calibration
calibration.titleNote: Results are shown for review — never written silently
calibration.subtitle: Calibrate the accelerometer once per build, the compass after any wiring or GPS-mast change.
calibration.principle: novaX principle: calibration never writes parameters without showing you first — every write is logged in Session Activity and reversible.

accel.idleBody: You'll place the vehicle in six orientations. Hold each one still until it's captured — about 90 seconds total.
accel.tableWarning: A wobbly capture ruins the result — use a table, not your hands.

hints.level: Place the vehicle flat and still
hints.left: Roll it onto its left side
hints.right: Roll it onto its right side

accel.failed: Calibration failed on the board. Nothing was written — you can start again.
accel.interrupted: Calibration was interrupted — the board disconnected mid-sequence. The result is incomplete and nothing is guaranteed either way; recheck INS_ACC* and redo all 6 faces once reconnected.

compass.idleBody: Rotate the vehicle slowly around all axes for about a minute. When sampling completes you'll review the new offsets before anything is written.
compass.learnDisclosure: Starting compass calibration sets COMPASS_LEARN=0 (in-flight compass learning disabled) — an implicit parameter write the FC makes on its own, separate from the offsets/diagonals/scale review gate below.
compass.samplingBody: Roll it nose-down, nose-up, on each side and flat — smooth, slow turns.
compass.reviewNote: Review before writing — nothing has been changed yet
compass.fitnessWarnBody: Fitness is higher than ideal for compass {{id}} — consider redoing this calibration away from metal or wiring.
compass.ackRejected: The board rejected the write — nothing was written. Safe to retry.
compass.confirmFailed: The write may have gone through but could not be verified — check the Parameters page before retrying.
compass.appliedBody: New offsets are on the board. Previous values are kept for this session, so you can roll back any time.
compass.failed: Compass calibration failed on the board (poor fit or bad orientation). Nothing was written — you can start again.
compass.interrupted: Compass calibration was interrupted — the board disconnected. Nothing was written yet; reconnect and recalibrate.

motors.notConnectedTitle: Motor test needs a connected board
motors.notConnectedBody: Connect the flight controller first. And while you're waiting — this is a good moment to take the props off.
motors.title: Motor Test
motors.titleNote: Props off · bench only · outputs capped at 30%
motors.subtitle: Verify motor order and spin direction against the diagram before first flight.
motors.frameUnknownNote: Frame not loaded yet — showing Quad X as a placeholder, not your real motor count.

steps.confirmPropsHint: Hard gate — nothing spins without it
steps.testHint: {{s}} s no-input · ESC · window blur · leaving page

safetyGate.title: SAFETY GATE
safetyGate.checkboxLabel: I confirm ALL propellers are removed
safetyGate.checkboxHint: …and the aircraft is secured so it can't move on the bench.

layout.title: MOTOR LAYOUT
layout.caption: Numbers = ArduPilot outputs · layout follows Setup → Frame

sliders.title: PER-MOTOR TEST
sliders.footnote: Sliders release to zero on any safety event. Output ceiling {{max}}% (MOT_TEST bench cap).

guide.title: Not sure of the wiring order?
guide.body: Guided motor-by-motor check: we spin one motor at a time through the same safety gate — you tell us which position actually spun. Mapping stays manual: fix SERVOx_FUNCTION yourself in Parameters if it doesn't match.
guide.spinning: Spinning position {{n}}… which motor actually moved?
guide.mismatch: Mismatch — expected position {{n}} to spin, but you confirmed position {{picked}}. Fix SERVOx_FUNCTION in Parameters.

banners.testActiveBody: auto-stop in {{s}} s without input · click STOP or press ESC · leaving this page stops all motors
banners.outputsEnabledBody: props must be off · auto-lock in {{s}} s if idle · window blur stops outputs

guide.title: First-flight Setup Guide
guide.subtitle: Five checks between a fresh board and a safe first hover. Progress is tracked for this session — skip anything you've done elsewhere.
guide.footerNote: Detection is read-only — the guide never changes parameters.

frameEsc.title: Frame & ESC protocol
frameEsc.descPending: Not read from the board yet

motorTest.title: Test motor order & direction
motorTest.descTodo: Props off — spin each motor briefly

failsafes.title: Failsafes
failsafes.desc: RC loss · low battery · GCS loss
